author: Austin Crawford
title: Seacrest Inn
summary: The Seacrest Inn has been completely renovated over the winter and has opened with a new and fresh look for the 2017 season! This 18 room inn is located on the corner of High Street and Weldon’s Way, just a short walk (under five minutes) from the Old Harbor Ferry landing. Each of the rooms has one queen bed,  A/C, all new furnishings, and private bathrooms. Other guest amenities include hairdryers, iron and ironing boards.  Guests of the Seacrest Inn check in first at the Harborside Inn, located directly across from the Old Harbor ferry landing. Check-in time is 3 pm and check-out time is 11 am. If you arrive before check-in or would like to spend the day on your departure day, we offer luggage storage at the Harborside Inn.
content:
The Seacrest Inn has been completely renovated over the winter and has opened with a new and fresh look for the 2017 season! This 18 room inn is located on the corner of High Street and Weldon’s Way, just a short walk (under five minutes) from the Old Harbor Ferry landing. Each of the rooms has one queen bed,  A/C, all new furnishings, and private bathrooms. Other guest amenities include hairdryers, iron and ironing boards.  Guests of the Seacrest Inn check in first at the Harborside Inn, located directly across from the Old Harbor ferry landing. Check-in time is 3 pm and check-out time is 11 am. If you arrive before check-in or would like to spend the day on your departure day, we offer luggage storage at the Harborside Inn.

All newly decorated and furnished rooms are equipped with one queen bed for a maximum of two guests. All rooms have a shower stall (no tub) and Direct TV. These rooms are located on the first and second floors. There is no elevator at the Seacrest Inn. Limited first come first serve parking is available upon request, although a vehicle will not be needed due to our convenient Old Harbor location.



 

Book Now & Rates

 




We require a 100% deposit on a Visa or Master Card or American Express for all reservations. Please note:  While we normally request a 50% deposit, we have changed our deposit policy in an effort to reduce in person contact on arrival due to the Covid-19 virus. 

Rentals will not include daily housekeeping for the 2020 season in an effort to reduce contact with our team. If you require trash pick up or fresh towels during your stay please contact our front desk office for new protocol.

We are amending our cancelation policies for hotel rooms, guest rooms and suites. We will require only one day's notice to cancel and receive your payment back less the $25 administrative fee. While we normally require six days notice to issue a refund for a reservation, we understand that in these uncertain times, changes may need to be made with less notice. 

We want to reiterate that if you are feeling ill at all, please postpone your visit to Block Island for your safety and ours. If you would like to cancel, please contact our office either by email or by phone no later than 3pm the day before you are scheduled to arrive. Within 24 hours of your check in time, refunds will not be issued. Refunds are not issued for late arrivals or early departures.  
Pets and smoking are not permitted at the Seacrest Inn.

title: Harbor Church
summary: Harbor Church is a warm community of faith which seeks to serve the entire island as followers of Jesus. We are affiliated with American Baptist Churches, but our membership includes persons of many denominations and we welcome visitors from all over to our services. Sunday morning Worship is always at 10:00 am year-round, followed by a coffee hour.  A Bible study for adults is held on Thursday at 7:00 pm. On Friday nights we host the Common Ground Coffeehouse with soup, coffee, and refreshments at 6:30, fellowship and entertainment (movies in winter) at 7:00 pm

title: Town of New Shoreham
summary: Find all the Town services for Block Island.  Here you will find the following departments:
content:
Find all the Town services for Block Island.  Here you will find the following departments:


BUILDING, ZONING, LAND USE AND PLANNING
Building Permits & Inspections, Land Development

FINANCE
Financial Accounting and Management, Payroll & Benefits, Water & Sewer Utility Billing, Tax Assessment & Collection

FIRE AND RESCUE
Refer to Block Island Volunteer Fire and Rescue under Boards, Committees and Commissions

HARBORS
Management of the Town's two harbor areas Old Harbor and New Harbor (Great Salt Pond), including shellfish licensing, mooring field management

 

Shellfish License Brochure

 

GIS / IT
Enterprise-wide development and management of the Town's Geographic Information System and information technology assets

LIBRARY
Library

POLICE
Police

PUBLIC WORKS
Responsible for Town Facilities and Roads

RECREATION
Year-round recreational programs, facilities and services for all ages in the community

TAX ASSESSMENT
Assessment of Real Estate, Tangible Property and Motor Vehicles, Administration of Personal Exemptions, and Production of the Town's Annual Tax Rolls

TAX COLLECTIONS
Collection of Property Taxes and Related Fees

TOWN CLERK
Town Council Actions, Ordinances, Licenses, Land Evidence Recording, Marriage Licenses, Birth, Death & Marriage Records, Voter Registration, Public Records, Probate Court, Elections and Town Meetings

title: Appleyard Guest House
summary: The “Appleyard” Boutique Guest House is located just a few minutes walk from the statue of Rebecca in the heart of the Old Harbor Village's Historic District with it's ferry landing and nearby beaches.
content:
The “Appleyard” Boutique Guest House is located just a few minutes walk from the statue of Rebecca in the heart of the Old Harbor Village's Historic District with it's ferry landing and nearby beaches.

Guestrooms, with private tiled bathrooms, have been redone and upgraded for the 2020 season. Each room is equipped with air conditioning, flat-screen Direct Television, complimentary WiFi, refrigerator, coffeemaker, and microwave for your complete privacy. There are no common rooms and the owner's quarters are completely separate. Enjoy an evening beverage on the spacious front porch or take out dining under the umbrella in our private garden. Off street parking, outdoor shower and a bike rack are available for all guests.

Rooms 1 and 3 are fitted with king size beds and room 2 with a queen. Adults preferred with no more than two guests per room. Come and enjoy the fresh air breezes of Block Island!!



 

Book Now & Rates

 


All guests of the Appleyard Guest House check-in and out at the Harborside Inn, located directly across from the Old Harbor Ferry Landing. From here we will give you directions to the Appleyard.  Check-in time is 4 pm. Check out time is 11 am. We offer complimentary luggage storage at the Harborside Inn if you arrive before check-in time or would like to spend the day on departure.

To reserve a room at the Appleyard Guest House we require a 100% deposit on a Visa or Master Card or American Express for two or more nights and 100% deposit for a single night. Please note:  While we normally request a 50% deposit, we have changed our deposit policy in an effort to reduce in-person contact on arrival due to the Covid-19 virus.

title: Lewis Farm Cottage
summary: Lewis Farm dates from 1817. Farming ended in 1964 and the family has since been at the forefront of the island’s open space movement. Lewis Farm sits in the middle of 400 acres of open space. The farm consists of seven dwelling units; five are occupied by family. The Building at Lewis Farm is the most secluded of all the dwellings.
content:
Lewis Farm dates from 1817. Farming ended in 1964 and the family has since been at the forefront of the island’s open space movement. Lewis Farm sits in the middle of 400 acres of open space. The farm consists of seven dwelling units; five are occupied by family. The Building at Lewis Farm is the most secluded of all the dwellings.

On Lewis Farm we herald the land. Our focus is the land and the outdoors. The accommodations are rustic but appropriately set in the middle of quiet, elegant views of meadows and ocean looking toward southwest ledge and Montauk. An island farmer grazes 20-30 head of cattle on 40 acres just to the west of the cottage. The west-facing deck is perfect for counting the cows, or the fishing boats.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Torrey Guest House
summary: This quaint summer cottage is located off of Payne Road in a quiet residential area under a mile from town and beaches. The Torrey Cottage has two bedrooms, one with a double bed and the second with two twins for a maximum of four guests.
content:
This quaint summer cottage is located off of Payne Road in a quiet residential area under a mile from town and beaches. The Torrey Cottage has two bedrooms, one with a double bed and the second with two twins for a maximum of four guests. 

There is a full kitchen (no dishwasher), living area with TV, one bathroom and an outside shower. Enjoy summer to it’s fullest while grilling on the deck after a beautiful Block Island beach day!  




 

Book Now & Rates

 



To reserve a week at the Torrey Cottage full payment is required at the time of booking. If you decide to cancel, your payment will be refunded less a $25 administrative fee, only if we are able to re-book your full vacation time in this rental to another guest. 

Bed linens and bath towels are included with this rental as well as cleaning on departure. There will be no daily housekeeping for the cottage.  

Check-in time is 4 pm at the Harborside Inn front desk (directly across from the Old Harbor Ferry landing). We will provide you with directions on arrival. Check out time is 11 am.

title: Water Street Suites
summary: The Water Street Suites are located behind the Water Street Inn convenient walking to the shops, restaurants and beaches. These suites are each equipped with a pull-out sofa, Direct TV, A/C, private bath, and either one or two bedrooms. Each bedroom has a queen size bed. content:
The Water Street Suites are located behind the Water Street Inn convenient walking to the shops, restaurants and beaches. These suites are each equipped with a pull-out sofa, Direct TV, A/C, private bath, and either one or two bedrooms. Each bedroom has a queen size bed. Two of the seven units are equipped with a stove for full cooking, while the remaining five have kitchenettes. The Water Street Suites are the perfect choice for families in a central location. Ask about our midweek Family Value Packages including ferry tickets, dining and bikes for four to six guests or click on specials to find out more.

Guests of the Water Street Suites may take advantage of the following complimentary amenities:  Indoor bike storage beneath the Harborside Inn (right next door). Luggage storage in the lobby of the Harborside Inn available prior to check-in 4 pm or after checking out 11 am. Beach towels are available with a deposit. The Water Street Suites have no private parking on the premises for vehicles. Due to the down town location a car is not needed nor recommended. Please keep in mind that the internet is unreliable on Block Island. If you are depending on internet during your time here the library offers the best connection.

This nonsmoking and pet free property is owner occupied. Loud gatherings and unregistered guests are not permitted. For groups renting these suites a security deposit will be due at check-in. This security will be fully refundable on your departure day as long as there is no damage, smoking, noise complaints or overloading of guests.  The Water Street Suites have a strict maximum capacity rule.



 

Book Now & Rates

 





There will be a $200 security deposit per suite charged to the credit card on file at check-in for the Water Street Suites. This security deposit will be fully refundable on departure day as long as there is no damage, the maximum capacity is not exceeded, and there is no excessive noise disrupting other guest’s stay.

A valid credit card and photo ID are required on arrival.

We require a 100% deposit on a Visa or Master Card or American Express for all reservations. Please note:  While we normally request a 50% deposit, we have changed our deposit policy in an effort to reduce in-person contact on arrival due to the Covid-19 virus. Rentals will not include daily housekeeping for the 2020 season in an effort to reduce contact with our team. If you require trash pick up or fresh towels during your stay please contact our front desk office for a new protocol

We are amending our cancelation policies for hotel rooms, guest rooms and suites. We will require only one day's notice to cancel and receive your payment back less than the $25 administrative fee. While we normally require six days' notice to issue a refund for a reservation, we understand that in these uncertain times, changes may need to be made with less notice. We want to reiterate that if you are feeling ill at all, please postpone your visit to Block Island for your safety and ours. If you would like to cancel, please contact our office either by email or by phone no later than 3 pm the day before you are scheduled to arrive. Within 24 hours of your check-in time, refunds will not be issued. Refunds are not issued for late arrivals or early departures.

Rates are per night and do not include tax of 13%. There is a two-night minimum stay at the weekend rate & three nights minimum on holiday weekends. There will be a 15% discount if rented for 7 or more nights. All posted rates are subject to change at any time.

content:
Block Island Accommodations is a magnificent collection of Bed & Breakfast, rental homes, and apartments on Block Island, RI, offering beautiful hilltop lodging and accommodations. Located just up High St you will find gracious hospitality, superior service, and comfort in a meadow-like setting overlooking the Atlantic.

Boating, fishing, and kayaking are all just a few minutes walk from our inns and homes.

We are open May through October. The ferry landings to the island are only a 2-hour drive from Boston and New York City and 45 minutes from Providence and Hartford. Our homes are all conveniently located via both car and foot from the ferry docks.

Our grounds are one of the best places on the island to just relax. Sit back in our Adirondack chairs, grill up lunch, and enjoy looking over the ocean. Most of our accommodations are only a short walk to town where you will find the shops, restaurants, movie theater, beach, as well as the best island nightlife.

There is so much to do and see throughout the summer months, from shopping, hiking, sailing, boating, parasailing, lighthouse tours, cruises, and fishing charters, that our accommodations offer a great central location for couples, groups and families alike

Live music is played nightly. Thursday through Sunday by Walter McDonough (an Irish folksinger from Galway who often will precede one of his songs with a quote from Irish poets like James Stephens or W.B. Yeats. Walter is real deal Irish and his music is as authentic as the man himself) and Monday through Wednesday by Izzy Malek (anyone familiar with the band Sugar will know him!). 

You’ll find Mahogany Shoals tucked away on Payne’s Dock in New Harbor. They are open all day but music usually starts about 10 pm. Payne’s Dock has a Facebook page where you can check out the current scene.

At the northern end of the island, you will find Sachem Pond. A mere half-mile from the North Lighthouse, it is the perfect spot to stop on your way to take a beach walk, bike ride, or watch the sunset. 

Block Island's National Wildlife Refuge surrounds Sachem Pond. Be mindful of sensitive dune edges where beach grass grows and nesting areas of any type within the refuge. Enjoy the beautiful scenery and wildlife that surrounds Sachem Pond.

If there is any time to visit Sachem Pond, it is most definitely Golden Hour. It's one of the most photographed places at sunset because of how the hues of the sun reflect off the water with the silhouette of the North Light in the background. A must-see!

author: Robert Lucier
title: Koru Eco Spa
summary: Back in 2007 when we began planning Koru we chose to use Block Island itself as our source of inspiration. Our goal was a laid-back and friendly island atmosphere rather than a stuffy and intimidating experience.
content:
Back in 2007 when we began planning Koru we chose to use Block Island itself as our source of inspiration. Our goal was a laid-back and friendly island atmosphere rather than a stuffy and intimidating experience.

*Get your nails done in our airy Gazebo overlooking the ocean or enjoy a massage in our well-appointed massage rooms; the Ocean Room and the Sunset Room.*

Perhaps most importantly, we wanted our services to be as pure and pristine as Block Island. That led us to look into using ingredients straight from nature in our services which then lead us down the path of discovering just how prevalent toxins are in the health and beauty industry. Once we discovered how much of our industry is awash in dangerous chemicals we committed to being an “eco” spa that would only offer services and sell products that we could confidently say met our standards of quality and safety. Our commitment still stands today and continues to carry thru in all of our business practices.

A great unwind activity on Block Island, we have something for everyone! Massage, Facials, Manicures and Pedicures and Hair Services as well as Wedding Styling!

summary: Explore Block Island with a great ride—style, ease, adventure!
content:
Explore Block Island with a great ride—style, ease, adventure!

Featured on Boston Chronicle 




Specializing in quality Trek and Raleigh bikes in a full range of sizes and a variety of models suited for avid cyclists, vacationing couples and families with children of all ages. Models include hybrids, fitness, sport-comfort, classic, cruiser, mountain, Fat-bikes, tandems, children’s bikes, tag-a-longs, and trailers. 



Take in the fresh sea air! Biking is the best way to see the island and arrive at sandy beaches, historic lighthouses, and greenway trails. Overlooking the fields of the Rose Farm, Beach Rose Bicycles is only a block from town on the way to the scenic Mohegan Bluffs and South East Lighthouse, making it an ideal location to begin your ride!

 

 

 


Peter and Jamie offer a friendly rental experience complete with directions. Our bikes are cleaned, maintained, and ready to ride! Most are accessorized with comfortable seats, bottle cages, and baskets to carry your beach-towels and gear. Helmets, locks, and maps are included, and we have water for sale.

Open everyday 8:00am-8:00pm.




Directions

From the ferry dock in town turn left onto Water Street. From the statue at the circle take a sharp right up to High Street and follow the sidewalk beside the movie theatre to the top of the hill. There will be signs for the Rose Farm Inn and Atlantic Inn. Follow the paved driveway past the two Inns. Our deck has a sign and red barn doors. 

Spring House Hotel guests only need to walk across the small field and through the opening in the split rail fence crossing our back lawn.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Harbors & Harbormaster
summary: Block Island maintains two harbors and is well-known as one of New England's premier boating destinations.
content:
Block Island maintains two harbors and is well-known as one of New England's premier boating destinations.

New Harbor is on the West Side of the Island, in the beautiful Great Salt Pond. New Harbor has 3 large marinas, 90 town moorings, and a 75-acre anchorage for vessels. The Harbormaster's Office is located at the Block Island Boat Basin during the summer season. 

For information on moorings and anchorage in New Harbor, please contact the Harbormaster on VHF Ch 12.

Moorings in New Harbor are first come first serve.  There is a $45 per night per boat fee and  a 50 cents per person landing fee will be assessed on the first night.  Cash, checks, Visa and MasterCard are accepted.

Old Harbor is on the East Side of the Island is a Harbor of Refuge protected by a breakwater.  There is limited space for anchoring which is restricted to seven days in a fourteen-day period. 

Dockage in Old Harbor is $3.00 per foot; day rates are $1.20 per foot.  You can contact the Dockmaster on VHF Ch 12 or at (401) 466-3235.

New Harbor Pumpout Boat on VHF Ch 73
Old Harbor Pumpout Boat on VHF Ch 72

New Harbor and Old Harbor have had a no discharge status since 1993.  Please utilize our free pump out boat service, the first of its kind on the East Coast.  New Harbor pump out service is available by contacting them on Ch 73 between 7:30 am and 5:00 pm.  Old Harbor pump out service is available on CH 72 from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm

Harbors office hours at the New Harbor Boat Basin (near the Oar Restaurant), where Shellfish Licenses may be obtained, are from 9:00 am to 3:00 pm seven days a week. 

VHF Radio - Ch12 - 7 am - 9 pm - All Calls 9 pm - 7 am - Emergency Only

Harbormaster: Kate McConville

To learn more about Shellfish Licenses, click below:

Lewis-Dickens Farm Wildlife Refuge is a coastal meadow offering gorgeous views of the Atlantic ocean. Lewis-Dickens Farm is 120 acres of conservation land, an excellent destination for walking, hiking, runners, and bird watchers.

Lewis-Dickens Farm preserved Block Island's farming history and was named after Elizabeth Dickens, once called the "Bird Lady of Block Island."
